items.headline: New Strong Sell Stocks for July 16th
items.snippet: ALB added to Zacks Rank #5 (Strong Sell) list; current-year earnings estimate revised 29.1% downward over the last 60 days.

items.headline: Top Stock Picks for Week of July 13, 2026
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation (ALB) is a premier specialty chemicals company with leading positions in attractive end markets globally. Earnings estimates for Albemarle for the second quarter of 2026 have been going up over the past month. The company should gain from long-term growth in

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month, underperforming the Zacks Chemical - Diversified industry and the S&P 500's declines of 6.7% and 0.1%, respectively. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately due to slowing EV deman

items.headline: Albemarle and Cars.com have been highlighted as Zacks Bull and Bear of the Day
items.snippet: ESS GWh production +117% YoY; global lithium consumption +37% YoY in February. Management affirmed FY26 outlook: $5.7-6.0B revenue, $2.4-2.6B adj. EBITDA, capex $550-600M.

items.headline: The U.S. Is Increasing Efforts to Rebuild Its Rare Earth Supply Chain
items.snippet: ALB is world's largest lithium producer; Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA jumped 148% YoY to $664M as lithium prices rebounded from 2025 lows; retired $1.3B of debt in the quarter.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: Stock pulled back to $140 range; analysts' average price target $214.65. Trading under 12x forward earnings, dividend yield 1.15%, payout ratio 46%.
